text:
The2024 Paris Olympic Gamesdraws to a close today following over two weeks of thrilling sporting action. A total of 329 medal events have been showcased during the event, which began with a spectacular display along the River Seine featuring athletes from all participating nations.

Thomas Jolly, the director of both the opening and closing ceremonies, has dubbed the concluding event 'Records', which will mark the end of a widely celebrated Games. Rumours of celebrity guests and an expected fireworks display hint at a grand celebration befitting the 17 days of athletic excellence witnessed in Paris and beyond.

But when does the closing ceremony actually start? And what TV channel will it be show on? Here's everything you need to know:

The closing ceremony is set to begin on Sunday at 8:00pm and is expected to last for two-and-a-half hours, finishing at around 10:30pm. For those planning to watch the entire show, director Jolly promises a spectacle, with 'world-renowned singers' slated to perform on the massive 2,800 square metre stage.

Snoop Dogg, who has been seen numerous times throughout the games cheering on Team USA, is rumoured to be among the performers.

UK viewers can tune intoBBCOne, BBC iPlayer and the BBC Sport website and app to catch the closing ceremony. The BBC's coverage will begin an hour earlier, at 7:00pm, providing ample time to build up to the event and reflect on the achievements of Team GB's competitors.



While it remains unconfirmed whetherHollywoodA-listerTom Cruisewill take part in the ceremony, rumours are circulating that he might pull off a daring stunt in true Mission: Impossible fashion. The presence of Cruise would symbolically hand over the reins to the United States, the host nation for the 2028 Games in Los Angeles.

If the star-studded opening ceremony - which saw performances from Lady Gaga, Celine Dion, and Aya Nakamura - is anything to go by, an appearance from the Top Gun actor wouldn't be particularly surprising.

The closing ceremony is set to unfold at the Stade de France. The venue has already seen action during these Games, hosting Rugby sevens matches and record-breaking athletics events, as well as the opening ceremony.

Highlights at the stadium included Armand 'Mondo' Duplantis' men's pole vault world record, Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone's 400m hurdles world best, and Keely Hodgkinson's 800m victory for Team GB.



We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

In stark contrast to the rain-soaked opening ceremony along the Seine, Sunday is predicted to be one of the hottest days of the year. According to weather reports, temperatures are expected to reach 33C in Paris during the ceremony, with the roofless Stade de France exposed to the elements.

Thankfully, no rainfall is forecasted for the event.

title: Paris Olympics closing ceremony: When does it start and what channel? | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Harry Brent
description: The 2024 Olympic Games in Paris is coming to an end this weekend, so here's everything you need to know about the closing ceremony
